name: Robb's Mississippi Cornbread
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F.
2. Place bacon drippings or Crisco in a 9-inch cast iron skillet.
3. Heat the skillet in the oven until the fat is smoking hot, while preparing the batter. (This should take no more than 5-10 minutes.)
4. In a medium bowl, thoroughly combine cornmeal, baking soda, and salt.
5. Add eggs and buttermilk all at once to the dry ingredients. Blend thoroughly using a wire whisk.
6. Slowly pour all but 1 tablespoon of hot fat into the batter, continuing to beat quickly with a whisk to incorporate the fat.
7. Turn the skillet around to coat the sides and bottom thoroughly with the remaining fat.
8. Pour the batter into the skillet.
9. Bake on the top rack of the oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the top of the cornbread is firm.
10. Turn the cornbread onto a plate and slip it back into the skillet upside-down. Return to the oven for 5 minutes.
11. Turn out onto a rack, cut into 6 wedges, and serve immediately.
Note: A cast-iron skillet is essential for a successful crust and moist bread.
